title: T-001 — Lock maintenance permission enum (mnt.*.* RBAC baseline, MNT-§4)
prompt:
# T-001 — Lock maintenance permission enum

PRD: docs/prd/13-MAINTENANCE-PRD.md §4 (Personas & RBAC), §14.2 (RLS write scopes)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Append a delimited MAINTENANCE permission group to `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` covering asset/mwo/pm/calibration/spare/sanitation/loto strings per PRD §4 RBAC matrix and Auditor A's P0 cross-cutting gap. Export `ALL_MAINTENANCE_PERMISSIONS`. Per Foundation §3 model is flat dot-namespaced strings (no derived matrix).

## Implementation contract
1. Append exactly these 17 strings (regex `^mnt\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$`) to `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` under a `MAINTENANCE_PERMISSIONS` group with JSDoc citing PRD §4:
   - `mnt.asset.read`, `mnt.asset.edit`, `mnt.asset.deactivate`
   - `mnt.mwo.request`, `mnt.mwo.approve`, `mnt.mwo.assign`, `mnt.mwo.execute`, `mnt.mwo.sign`, `mnt.mwo.cancel`
   - `mnt.pm.create`, `mnt.pm.skip`
   - `mnt.calib.record`, `mnt.calib.upload_cert`
   - `mnt.spare.consume`, `mnt.spare.adjust`, `mnt.spare.reorder`
   - `mnt.loto.apply`, `mnt.loto.clear`
2. Export `ALL_MAINTENANCE_PERMISSIONS` as a typed `Permission[]` array literal so vitest can iterate.
3. Add vitest covering: (a) presence of all 17 strings, (b) regex `^mnt\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$` match, (c) no duplicates across the full enum, (d) CODEOWNERS still architect-locks the file.

## Files
- packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts [modify]
- packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts [modify]
- CODEOWNERS [modify]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the enum file is read, when imported, then it exposes exactly the 17 strings listed in §Implementation contract under a `MAINTENANCE_PERMISSIONS` group.
2. Given a permission key matches `^mnt\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$`, when the enum is parsed, then no duplicates exist (vitest assertion across full Permission union).
3. Given the file is changed, when CODEOWNERS is read, then `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` remains architect-locked.
4. Given `ALL_MAINTENANCE_PERMISSIONS` is imported, when iterated, then it returns exactly 17 entries typed `Permission[]`.

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED: extend permissions.test.ts asserting the 17 strings + regex + uniqueness; run vitest, expect failure.
- GREEN: append strings + export; re-run.
- Typecheck + lint must pass.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/rbac vitest run packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts && pnpm typecheck`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not remove or rename any existing permission string (would break ESLint enum-lock guard from Foundation T-046).
- Do not introduce a parallel matrix model (Foundation §3 [D2] flat model only).
- Do not weaken CODEOWNERS coverage.
- Do not add Settings/Quality/Production-namespaced perms in this task — out of scope.
